meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve Project Stall
content.seo_description: Stop leaving projects unfinished. Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your first real-world SaaS app as a student.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of structured delivery. By adopting a professional client workflow, you treat your side projects like high-stakes contracts. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution to maintain velocity, ensure code quality, and cross the finish line every time.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is paved with good intentions. You start with high energy, only to hit the 'feature creep' wall or the crushing silence of an unorganized codebase. It is the classic student trap: you have the technical curiosity, but you lack the structural discipline to turn a prototype into a product.

But what if you treated your own project like a high-paying client assignment? By shifting your mindset to a formal client workflow, you trade endless tinkering for measurable milestones. This guide shows you how to leverage modern AI tools to maintain momentum and ship your project from concept to completion.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Establish clear boundaries for your project to prevent feature creep. Define exactly what 'done' looks like before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior software architect. Help me define a minimal viable product scope for [Project Name]. List exactly 3 core features that provide maximum value. Exclude all 'nice-to-have' features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a project roadmap file named ROADMAP.md based on the 3 core features defined.'

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: Solid foundations prevent refactoring later. Use AI to generate a scalable schema that supports your MVP goals.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for my MVP. Use Prisma or SQL syntax. Ensure it supports user authentication and the 3 core features identified in step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create schema.prisma file and run migration to initialize the database.'

steps.title: Build the Core UI Layout
steps.description: Focus on the primary user flow. Don't worry about styling yet; focus on functionality and component structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a clean, responsive component structure for the main application dashboard using Tailwind CSS and React components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the layout and primary navigation components based on the architecture.'

steps.title: Implement API Logic
steps.description: Connect the frontend to the backend. This is where your app moves from static pages to a dynamic application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the API route handlers for my core features. Ensure error handling and input validation are included.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the API routes in the /api folder and connect them to the database service.'

steps.title: Quality Assurance and Deployment
steps.description: Run final tests and push to production to finalize your project milestone.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist of 5 essential tests to perform on my app before deployment to ensure core functionality works.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run test suite and generate a deployment summary for Vercel.'

pro_tips: Always commit your changes after every completed feature; it creates a psychological 'win' streak.
Use a 'Dead-End' folder for experimental code; never let experiments pollute your main production branch.
Treat your README.md as a client progress report; if you can't explain the feature in the README, it's too complex.
Limit your development sessions to 90-minute blocks; the 'Pomodoro' technique applied to coding prevents burnout.
Prioritize data flow over UI aesthetics; a functional, ugly app is infinitely more valuable than a beautiful, broken one.
content.next_steps: After completing your first project, document the process in a blog post or portfolio case study. This 'build-in-public' approach is the #1 way to get hired as a developer.
content.conversion_block: You now have the workflow to stop starting and start finishing. Don't let your next idea become another forgotten folder. Take this structure, apply it to your passion project, and ship it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
